devanagari: गोपानां गोकुलं परित्यज्य वृन्दावने गमनं तत्र । श्रीकृष्णद्वारावत्सासुरबकासुरयोर्वधः । श्रीशुक उवाच । गोपा नन्दादयः श्रुत्वा द्रुमयोः पत...
english_devnagari: gopānāṃ gokulaṃ parityajya vṛndāvane gamanaṃ tatraśrīkṛṣṇadvārāvatsāsurabakāsurayorvadhaḥśrīśuka uvācagopā nandādayaḥ śrutvā drumayoḥ patato ravamtatrājagmuḥ kuruśreṣṭha nirghātabhayaśaṅkitāḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the crashing sound of the falling (Arjuna) trees, cowherds headed by Nanda (thinking it to be a roar of thunder) rushed to the spot, afraid as they were of a thunderbolt, Oh prominent Kuru.

devanagari: भूम्यां निपतितौ तत्र ददृशुर्यमलार्जुनौ । बभ्रमुस्तदविज्ञाय लक्ष्यं पतनकारणम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmyāṃ nipatitau tatra dadṛśuryamalārjunaubabhramustadavijñāya lakṣyaṃ patanakāraṇam
translation: They saw the twin Arjuna trees fallen to the ground They were bewildered as they could not guess the cause of that fall which was quite obvious.

devanagari: उलूखलं विकर्षन्तं दाम्ना बद्धं च बालकम् । कस्येदं कुत आश्चर्यं उत्पात इति कातराः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ulūkhalaṃ vikarṣantaṃ dāmnā baddhaṃ ca bālakamkasyedaṃ kuta āścaryaṃ utpāta iti kātarāḥ
translation: It was the child who was dragging the wooden mortar tied to it with a cord. (But not believing in it) they got distressed as to who did it, what could be the cause of that wonder or whether it is an evil portent.

devanagari: बाला ऊचुरनेनेति तिर्यग्गतं उलूखलम् । विकर्षता मध्यगेन पुरुषौ अपि अचक्ष्महि ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: bālā ūcuraneneti tiryaggataṃ ulūkhalamvikarṣatā madhyagena puruṣau api acakṣmahi
translation: The boys (who were present there) reported, “It (the uprooting of the trees) has been done by him (Kṛṣṇa) who going between the trees dragged the mortar, which lay athwart the trees. We also saw two divine figures (rising from the fallen trees)

devanagari: न ते तदुक्तं जगृहुः न घटेतेति तस्य तत् । बालस्योत्पाटनं तर्वोः केचित् संदिग्धचेतसः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: na te taduktaṃ jagṛhuḥ na ghaṭeteti tasya tatbālasyotpāṭanaṃ tarvoḥ kecit saṃdigdhacetasaḥ
translation: They did not believe in the report of the boys. The sceptics reasoned with themselves the impossibility of uprooting the two big trees by the child, while a few others had some reservations in their minds (as they remembered Kṛṣṇa’s previous feats).

devanagari: उलूखलं विकर्षन्तं दाम्ना बद्धं स्वमात्मजम् । विलोक्य नन्दः प्रहसद् वदनो विमुमोच ह ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ulūkhalaṃ vikarṣantaṃ dāmnā baddhaṃ svamātmajamvilokya nandaḥ prahasad vadano vimumoca ha
translation: It is traditionally reported that seeing his own child tethered with a cord and dragging the mortar, Nanda with his countenance beaming with a smile, set him free.

devanagari: गोपीभिः स्तोभितोऽनृत्यद् भगवान् बालवत् क्वचित् । उद्‍गायति क्वचिन्मुग्धः तद्वशो दारुयन्त्रवत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: gopībhiḥ stobhito'nṛtyad bhagavān bālavat kvacitud‍gāyati kvacinmugdhaḥ tadvaśo dāruyantravat
translation: Glorious Lord (of the Universe) though he was, he sometime danced like a child, sometimes sang loudly and behaved like a wooden doll under the control of the Gopīs (cowherd women), when they encouraged him with their clapping of hands and coaxings.

devanagari: बिभर्ति क्वचिदाज्ञप्तः पीठकोन्मानपादुकम् । बाहुक्षेपं च कुरुते स्वानां च प्रीतिमावहन् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: bibharti kvacidājñaptaḥ pīṭhakonmānapādukambāhukṣepaṃ ca kurute svānāṃ ca prītimāvahan
translation: Commanded by them, sometimes he would bring articles like a wooden stool, a measure or a pair of wooden sandals, and sometimes he would swing up his arms bringing delight and affection to his own people (his devotees).

devanagari: दर्शयंस्तद्विदां लोक आत्मनो भृत्यवश्यताम् । व्रजस्योवाह वै हर्षं भगवान् बालचेष्टितैः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: darśayaṃstadvidāṃ loka ātmano bhṛtyavaśyatāmvrajasyovāha vai harṣaṃ bhagavān bālaceṣṭitaiḥ
translation: To the people who recognize him (as God), he, thereby demonstrates his subservience to (the will of) his servants. The glorious Lord thus brought great joy to (i.e. in the life of) people in Vraja through his child-like playful activities.

devanagari: क्रीणीहि भोः फलानीति श्रुत्वा सत्वरमच्युतः । फलार्थी धान्यमादाय ययौ सर्वफलप्रदः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: krīṇīhi bhoḥ phalānīti śrutvā satvaramacyutaḥphalārthī dhānyamādāya yayau sarvaphalapradaḥ
translation: [1] Hearing the vendor’s cry, “Oh people, purchase fruits”, Kṛṣṇa rushed to him quickly with food grains (in the hollow of his palms to give in exchange of fruit), desirous of getting the fruits, though he himself is the bestower of fruits (of actions, prayers, etc.) to all.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Verses 10-20, both inclusive, are not commented upon by Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Subodhinī (as they obviously do not recognize them as a part of the text), but Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī, VT., Kramasandarbha., VC. have recognized them as an integral part of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa and have annotated on them. VT. notes that though there are variations in the order of these verses, the Gauḍīya Sampradāya text is followed.
Though it is not possible to present a concordance of the various text-traditions of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa, the complexity of the problem can be indicated by comparing Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Padaratnāvalī’s text here. Ch. XI in Bhāvāratha Dīpikā is Ch. XII in Padaratnāvalī In this Chapter after the 9th verse, the order of vv in Padaratnāvalī is as follows:
I. Vv 10-18.
21-29 (in Bhāvāratha Dīpikā): From gopa-vṛddhā mahot-pātān...upto bhavatāṃ yadi rocate /
II. 19(A). (extra verse)
tac chrutvaika-dhiyo gopāḥ sarve nanda purogamāḥ /
upa-nanda-vaco rājan pratinandya gṛhān yayuḥ // 19.—Vs. 20-28
III. 12-20 (in Bhāvāratha Dīpikā):
sarit-tīragataṃ kṛṣṇaṃ kṛtavatyath?d0?y?m (?)
IV. 29A. (Additional verse):
atha śandaḥ samāhūya gopānidam abhāṣata /
śveto vṛndāvanaṃ yāmo yatā bha?atha mā????m /
V. The remaining 30 verses from tac chruttvaikadhiyā gopāḥ sādhu sādhviti vādinaḥ / etc. are common to Bhāvāratha Dīpikā & Padaratnāvalī This is the XII ch. in Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे एकादशोऽध्यायः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe ekādaśo'dhyāyaḥ
translation: The fruit-seller-woman filled with fruits (the hollow of the) palms of his hands from which grains were slipping down (through the gap between them) and the basket of fruits was filled with precious stones.

devanagari: फलविक्रयिणी तस्य च्युतधान्यकरद्वयम् । फलैरपूरयद् रत्‍नैः फलभाण्डमपूरि च । सरित्तीरगतं कृष्णं भग्नार्जुनमथाह्वयत् । रामं च रोहिणी देवी ...
english_devnagari: phalavikrayiṇī tasya cyutadhānyakaradvayamphalairapūrayad rat‍naiḥ phalabhāṇḍamapūri casarittīragataṃ kṛṣṇaṃ bhagnārjunamathāhvayatrāmaṃ ca rohiṇī devī krīḍantaṃ bālakairbhṛśam
translation: On one occasion, queen Rohiṇī called (Bala-) Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, the uprooter of the Arjuna trees, who were engrossed in playing with children on the bank of the river.

devanagari: नोपेयातां यदाऽहूतौ क्रीडासङ्गेन पुत्रकौ । यशोदां प्रेषयामास रोहिणी पुत्रवत्सलाम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: nopeyātāṃ yadā'hūtau krīḍāsaṅgena putrakauyaśodāṃ preṣayāmāsa rohiṇī putravatsalām
translation: When both the sons being deeply interested in play did not come back despite her calling, Rohiṇī sent Yaśodā who was so much affectionate to her child.

devanagari: क्रीडन्तं सा सुतं बालैः अतिवेलं सहाग्रजम् । यशोदाजोहवीत् कृष्णं पुत्रस्नेहस्नुतस्तनी ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: krīḍantaṃ sā sutaṃ bālaiḥ ativelaṃ sahāgrajamyaśodājohavīt kṛṣṇaṃ putrasnehasnutastanī
translation: Yaśodā, with her breasts overflowing with affection for her child, repeatedly shouted for her child who along with his elder brother was playing with the children beyond the (usual) time-limit.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्णारविन्दाक्ष तात एहि स्तनं पिब । अलं विहारैः क्षुत्क्षान्तः क्रीडाश्रान्तोऽसि पुत्रक ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇāravindākṣa tāta ehi stanaṃ pibaalaṃ vihāraiḥ kṣutkṣāntaḥ krīḍāśrānto'si putraka
translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa of lotus-eyes! Child! Come up and suck at my bosom. Enough of playing. You must be feeling fatigued with hunger and exhausted with exertions in playing, my sonny.

devanagari: हे रामागच्छ ताताशु सानुजः कुलनन्दन । प्रातरेव कृताहारः तद् भवान् भोक्तुमर्हति ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: he rāmāgaccha tātāśu sānujaḥ kulanandanaprātareva kṛtāhāraḥ tad bhavān bhoktumarhati
translation: Oh Rāma! Come quickly with your younger brother. Oh child. It is very early in the morning that you had a breakfast. You must take food now, Oh delight of the family.

devanagari: प्रतीक्षते त्वां दाशार्ह भोक्ष्यमाणो व्रजाधिपः । एह्यावयोः प्रियं धेहि स्वगृहान् यात बालकाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: pratīkṣate tvāṃ dāśārha bhokṣyamāṇo vrajādhipaḥehyāvayoḥ priyaṃ dhehi svagṛhān yāta bālakāḥ
translation: Oh Dāśārha! Nanda, the head of this Vraja who is about to take his meals is waiting for you. Do come and bring joy to us both. Oh boys! You return to your houses.

devanagari: धूलिधूसरिताङ्गस्त्वं पुत्र मज्जनमावह । जन्मर्क्षमद्य भवतो विप्रेभ्यो देहि गाः शुचिः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhūlidhūsaritāṅgastvaṃ putra majjanamāvahajanmarkṣamadya bhavato viprebhyo dehi gāḥ śuciḥ
translation: Your body has been covered with dust, Oh child! Go and take your bath. The star, presiding over your birth, is in the ascent. Get purified by bath, and give cows to Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: पश्य पश्य वयस्यांस्ते मातृमृष्टान् स्वलङ्कृतान् । त्वं च स्नातः कृताहारो विहरस्व स्वलङ्कृतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: paśya paśya vayasyāṃste mātṛmṛṣṭān svalaṅkṛtāntvaṃ ca snātaḥ kṛtāhāro viharasva svalaṅkṛtaḥ
translation: Behold your play-mates. They have been washed and well-adorned by their mothers. You also take your bath, have your meals, and after getting well-adorned, go to play.”

devanagari: इत्थं यशोदा तमशेषशेखरं । मत्वा सुतं स्नेहनिबद्धधीर्नृप । हस्ते गृहीत्वा सहराममच्युतं । नीत्वा स्ववाटं कृतवत्यथोदयम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ yaśodā tamaśeṣaśekharaṃmatvā sutaṃ snehanibaddhadhīrnṛpahaste gṛhītvā saharāmamacyutaṃnītvā svavāṭaṃ kṛtavatyathodayam
translation: In this way, Yaśodā whose mind was bound with the ties of maternal affection, regarded, Kṛṣṇa, the crown of all as her child. She took both Rāma and Kṛṣṇa by hand, and brought them to her house, and made them perform the auspicious rites.

devanagari: गोपवृद्धा महोत्पातान् अनुभूय बृहद्वने । नन्दादयः समागम्य व्रजकार्यं अमन्त्रयन् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: gopavṛddhā mahotpātān anubhūya bṛhadvanenandādayaḥ samāgamya vrajakāryaṃ amantrayan
translation:
Śuka said:
Observing extremely ominous portents in the Bṛhadvana (Gokula), elderly Gopas (cowherds) like Nanda and others met in a conference and deliberated the measures to be adopted in the interest of Vraja.

devanagari: तत्र उपनन्दनामाऽह गोपो ज्ञानवयोऽधिकः । देशकालार्थतत्त्वज्ञः प्रियकृद् रामकृष्णयोः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra upanandanāmā'ha gopo jñānavayo'dhikaḥdeśakālārthatattvajñaḥ priyakṛd rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ
translation: Out of them, a cowherd called Upananda, who was senior to others in wisdom and age, and who was endowed with a penetrating insight into the exigencies of the time, place and things (situation), and a well-wisher of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa advised as follows:

devanagari: उत्थातव्यं इतोऽस्माभिः गोकुलस्य हितैषिभिः । आयान्ति अत्र महोत्पाता बालानां नाशहेतवः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: utthātavyaṃ ito'smābhiḥ gokulasya hitaiṣibhiḥāyānti atra mahotpātā bālānāṃ nāśahetavaḥ
translation: “We, the well-wishers of Gokula should now migrate from this place; for, terrible portents of an evil nature forboding destruction of our children are visiting this place.

devanagari: मुक्तः कथञ्चिद् राक्षस्या बालघ्न्या बालको ह्यसौ । हरेरनुग्रहात् नूनं अनश्चोपरि नापतत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: muktaḥ kathañcid rākṣasyā bālaghnyā bālako hyasauhareranugrahāt nūnaṃ anaścopari nāpatat
translation: This child Kṛṣṇa certainly had a miraculous escape with great difficulty from the ogress (Pūtanā), the slayer of children. It was surely through the grace of Lord Hari, that the cart did not fall on Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: चक्रवातेन नीतोऽयं दैत्येन विपदं वियत् । शिलायां पतितस्तत्र परित्रातः सुरेश्वरैः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: cakravātena nīto'yaṃ daityena vipadaṃ viyatśilāyāṃ patitastatra paritrātaḥ sureśvaraiḥ
translation: He was carried away to the skies, the region of birds, by a demon in the form of a whirlwind. He fell on a rock, but was protected by great gods (our deities).

devanagari: यन्न म्रियेत द्रुमयोः अन्तरं प्राप्य बालकः । असौ अन्यतमो वापि तदप्यच्युतरक्षणम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yanna mriyeta drumayoḥ antaraṃ prāpya bālakaḥasau anyatamo vāpi tadapyacyutarakṣaṇam
translation: It is sheerly due to the protection afforded by Lord Acyuta that this child or any other boy should not die on getting in between the (Arjuna) trees (as they crashed).

devanagari: यावत् औत्पातिकोऽरिष्टो व्रजं नाभिभवेदितः । तावद् बालानुपादाय यास्यामोऽन्यत्र सानुगाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvat autpātiko'riṣṭo vrajaṃ nābhibhaveditaḥtāvad bālānupādāya yāsyāmo'nyatra sānugāḥ
translation: Before Vraja is not overtaken by any calamity indicated by these evil portents, let us repair to some other place along with our children and followers and attendants.

devanagari: वनं वृन्दावनं नाम पशव्यं नवकाननम् । गोपगोपीगवां सेव्यं पुण्याद्रि तृणवीरुधम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vanaṃ vṛndāvanaṃ nāma paśavyaṃ navakānanamgopagopīgavāṃ sevyaṃ puṇyādri tṛṇavīrudham
translation: There is a virgin forest tract called Vṛndāvana which along with its sacred hills, pasturage, trees and plants, is suitable to our cattle and fit to be a happy settlement to our milkmen, women and cattle.

devanagari: तत्तत्राद्यैव यास्यामः शकटान् युङ्क्त मा चिरम् । गोधनान्यग्रतो यान्तु भवतां यदि रोचते ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tattatrādyaiva yāsyāmaḥ śakaṭān yuṅkta mā ciramgodhanānyagrato yāntu bhavatāṃ yadi rocate
translation:
Let us, therefore, shift there, this very day. Get our carts and vehicles ready. Be quick (No delay please). If you so approve of it, let our wealth, the herds of cattle, go ahead in advance.”
[Padaratnāvalī’s text adds:]
29(A). Having heard the speech of Upananda with concentration of mind (and attentively), all the cowherds of which Nanda was the leader, approved of it, and repaired to their houses, Oh King.
29(B). Then Nanda summoned together all the Gopas and directed (spoke) as follows:
“Tomorrow we shall go from this place to Vṛndāvana. Be prepared. Don’t delay.”

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वैकधियो गोपाः साधु साध्विति वादिनः । व्रजान् स्वान् स्वान् समायुज्य ययू रूढपरिच्छदाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvaikadhiyo gopāḥ sādhu sādhviti vādinaḥvrajān svān svān samāyujya yayū rūḍhaparicchadāḥ
translation: Unanimously approving his speech, all Gopas exclaimed “That’s right! Very good”. They got their respective conveyances ready with their effects, packed and loaded.

devanagari: वृद्धान् बालान् स्त्रियो राजन् सर्वोपकरणानि च । अनःस्वारोप्य गोपाला यत्ता आत्तशरासनाः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛddhān bālān striyo rājan sarvopakaraṇāni caanaḥsvāropya gopālā yattā āttaśarāsanāḥ
translation: Having put into their carriages the aged persons, young children and their women-folk along with utensils and other accessories, the Gopas who were all alert carried bows already strung, in their hands.

devanagari: गोधनानि पुरस्कृत्य शृङ्गाण्यापूर्य सर्वतः । तूर्यघोषेण महता ययुः सहपुरोहिताः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: godhanāni puraskṛtya śṛṅgāṇyāpūrya sarvataḥtūryaghoṣeṇa mahatā yayuḥ sahapurohitāḥ
translation: Keeping their wealth of cattle in their front in advance, accompanied by their family priests, they started on their journey with a flourish of blowing horns and sounding trumpets on all sides.

devanagari: गोप्यो रूढरथा नूत्‍न कुचकुंकुम कान्तयः । कृष्णलीला जगुः प्रीत्या निष्ककण्ठ्यः सुवाससः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: gopyo rūḍharathā nūt‍na kucakuṃkuma kāntayaḥkṛṣṇalīlā jaguḥ prītyā niṣkakaṇṭhyaḥ suvāsasaḥ
translation: Mounted on chariots, the Gopa women of lovely complexion, with fresh application of saffron-paint to their breasts, wore strings of gold coins round their necks, and put on excellent garments, they felt supremely happy and sang the sportive acts of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तथा यशोदारोहिण्यौ एकं शकटमास्थिते । रेजतुः कृष्णरामाभ्यां तत्कथाश्रवणोत्सुके ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā yaśodārohiṇyau ekaṃ śakaṭamāsthiterejatuḥ kṛṣṇarāmābhyāṃ tatkathāśravaṇotsuke
translation: So also Yaśodā and Rohiṇī occupied the same carriage, and were eager to hear the sportive acts of their dear children, shone (brightly) with Kṛṣṇa and Rāma (on their laps).

devanagari: वृन्दावनं सम्प्रविश्य सर्वकालसुखावहम् । तत्र चक्रुर्व्रजावासं शकटैः अर्धचन्द्रवत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛndāvanaṃ sampraviśya sarvakālasukhāvahamtatra cakrurvrajāvāsaṃ śakaṭaiḥ ardhacandravat
translation: Entering Vṛndāvana which was pleasant in all seasons, they, with a crescent-shaped formation of their vehicles, set up the habitation (for them and the cattle), semicircular like unto the half disc of the moon.

devanagari: वृन्दावनं गोवर्धनं यमुनापुलिनानि च । वीक्ष्यासीत् उत्तमा प्रीती राममाधवयोर्नृप ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛndāvanaṃ govardhanaṃ yamunāpulināni cavīkṣyāsīt uttamā prītī rāmamādhavayornṛpa
translation: Beholding the forest of Vṛndāvana, the Govardhana hill (nearby), and the sand-banks of the Yamunā, the hearts of Rāma and Kṛṣṇa surged with great joy.

devanagari: एवं व्रजौकसां प्रीतिं यच्छन्तौ बालचेष्टितैः । कलवाक्यैः स्वकालेन वत्सपालौ बभूवतुः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vrajaukasāṃ prītiṃ yacchantau bālaceṣṭitaiḥkalavākyaiḥ svakālena vatsapālau babhūvatuḥ
translation: In this way, giving delight to the inhabitants of Vraja with their child-like sportive activities and sweet lisping, they, in due course, began to tend calves.

devanagari: अविदूरे व्रजभुवः सह गोपालदारकैः । चारयामासतुः वत्सान् नानाक्रीडापरिच्छदौ ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: avidūre vrajabhuvaḥ saha gopāladārakaiḥcārayāmāsatuḥ vatsān nānākrīḍāparicchadau
translation: In the vicinity of their habitation, and in the company of cowherd boys, they tended the calves, taking with them various articles of play.

devanagari: क्वचिद् वादयतो वेणुं क्षेपणैः क्षिपतः क्वचित् । क्वचित् पादैः किङ्किणीभिः क्वचित् कृत्रिमगोवृषैः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid vādayato veṇuṃ kṣepaṇaiḥ kṣipataḥ kvacitkvacit pādaiḥ kiṅkiṇībhiḥ kvacit kṛtrimagovṛṣaiḥ
translation: Sometimes they played on flutes. Sometimes they used fruits of the Bilva tree or of the Emblic myrobalan (āmalaka) for throwing as balls; sometimes they played kicking those with their feet, making a tinkling noise of small bells (of the anklets); sometimes they imitated the cattle with two- three boys as the tender of cattle, sometime they played the part of bellowing and fighting bulls. Sometimes they imitated the cries of birds like swans and peacocks. In this way they roved simulating ordinary children.

devanagari: वृषायमाणौ नर्दन्तौ युयुधाते परस्परम् । अनुकृत्य रुतैर्जन्तून् चेरतुः प्राकृतौ यथा ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṣāyamāṇau nardantau yuyudhāte parasparamanukṛtya rutairjantūn ceratuḥ prākṛtau yathā
translation: Sometimes they played on flutes. Sometimes they used fruits of the Bilva tree or of the Emblic myrobalan (āmalaka) for throwing as balls; sometimes they played kicking those with their feet, making a tinkling noise of small bells (of the anklets); sometimes they imitated the cattle with two- three boys as the tender of cattle, sometime they played the part of bellowing and fighting bulls. Sometimes they imitated the cries of birds like swans and peacocks. In this way they roved simulating ordinary children.

devanagari: कदाचिद् यमुनातीरे वत्सान् चारयतोः स्वकैः । वयस्यैः कृष्णबलयोः जिघांसुर्दैत्य आगमत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kadācid yamunātīre vatsān cārayatoḥ svakaiḥvayasyaiḥ kṛṣṇabalayoḥ jighāṃsurdaitya āgamat
translation: On one occasion, while both Rāma and Kṛṣṇa were pasturing the calves along with their friends, a demon came there, with the intention of killing Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma.

devanagari: तं वत्सरूपिणं वीक्ष्य वत्सयूथगतं हरिः । दर्शयन् बलदेवाय शनैर्मुग्ध इवासदत् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vatsarūpiṇaṃ vīkṣya vatsayūthagataṃ hariḥdarśayan baladevāya śanairmugdha ivāsadat
translation: On seeing him assume the form of a calf and mix with the herd of calves, he (Kṛṣṇa) pointed him out to Balarāma, and slowly approached the demon, posing as if he knew nothing about him.

devanagari: गृहीत्वा अपरपादाभ्यां सहलाङ्गूलमच्युतः । भ्रामयित्वा कपित्थाग्रे प्राहिणोद् गतजीवितम् । स कपित्थैर्महाकायः पात्यमानैः पपात ह ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītvā aparapādābhyāṃ sahalāṅgūlamacyutaḥbhrāmayitvā kapitthāgre prāhiṇod gatajīvitamsa kapitthairmahākāyaḥ pātyamānaiḥ papāta ha
translation: Catching hold of him by his hind legs and tail, and whirling him round, he hurled him lifeless to the top of the wood-apple (Kapittha) tree. The huge-bodied demon (who assumed his demonic form at the time of death) fell down along with wood-apples which were made to fall (with the impact of the huge dead body).

devanagari: तं वीक्ष्य विस्मिता बालाः शशंसुः साधु साध्विति । देवाश्च परिसन्तुष्टा बभूवुः पुष्पवर्षिणः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vīkṣya vismitā bālāḥ śaśaṃsuḥ sādhu sādhvitidevāśca parisantuṣṭā babhūvuḥ puṣpavarṣiṇaḥ
translation: Astonished to see him fall, all children cried out, “Bravo! Well-done!” Gods being highly delighted, showered flowers.

devanagari: तौ वत्सपालकौ भूत्वा सर्वलोकैकपालकौ । सप्रातराशौ गोवत्सान् चारयन्तौ विचेरतुः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tau vatsapālakau bhūtvā sarvalokaikapālakausaprātarāśau govatsān cārayantau viceratuḥ
translation: The sole protectors of all the worlds became the two tenders of calves, and with the breakfast in their wallet, roamed about leading the calves in their charge to pasture.

devanagari: स्वं स्वं वत्सकुलं सर्वे पाययिष्यन्त एकदा । गत्वा जलाशयाभ्याशं पाययित्वा पपुर्जलम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: svaṃ svaṃ vatsakulaṃ sarve pāyayiṣyanta ekadāgatvā jalāśayābhyāśaṃ pāyayitvā papurjalam
translation: On one occasion, all the cowherd boys wanted to take their respective herd of calves to water. They went to a tank[2] of water, made the calves to drink and they also drank of it.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
VT. locates it to the east of the Nandīśvara hill, near the spot now known as baka-sthala.

devanagari: ते तत्र ददृशुर्बाला महासत्त्वमवस्थितम् । तत्रसुर्वज्रनिर्भिन्नं गिरेः शृङ्गमिव च्युतम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: te tatra dadṛśurbālā mahāsattvamavasthitamtatrasurvajranirbhinnaṃ gireḥ śṛṅgamiva cyutam
translation: The boys saw there standing a monstrous creature like unto a peak of a mountain sundered by (Indra’s) thunderbolt, and slipped (of the mountain) and they got terrified.

devanagari: स वै बको नाम महानसुरो बकरूपधृक् । आगत्य सहसा कृष्णं तीक्ष्णतुण्डोऽग्रसद्‍बली ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai bako nāma mahānasuro bakarūpadhṛkāgatya sahasā kṛṣṇaṃ tīkṣṇatuṇḍo'grasad‍balī
translation: It was verily a powerful demon called Baka who assumed the form of a stork. Swooping down suddenly, the powerful monster with a sharp beak, swallowed up Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कृष्णं महाबकग्रस्तं दृष्ट्वा रामादयोऽर्भकाः । बभूवुरिन्द्रियाणीव विना प्राणं विचेतसः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇaṃ mahābakagrastaṃ dṛṣṭvā rāmādayo'rbhakāḥbabhūvurindriyāṇīva vinā prāṇaṃ vicetasaḥ
translation: Perceiving Kṛṣṇa swallowed up by a big crane, children like Balarāma (but not Balarāma) became stupefied like organs and senses of the body in the absence of the vital breath.

devanagari: तं तालुमूलं प्रदहन्तमग्निवद् । गोपालसूनुं पितरं जगद्‍गुरोः । चच्छर्द सद्योऽतिरुषाक्षतं बकः । तुण्डेन हन्तुं पुनरभ्यपद्यत ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tālumūlaṃ pradahantamagnivadgopālasūnuṃ pitaraṃ jagad‍guroḥcaccharda sadyo'tiruṣākṣataṃ bakaḥtuṇḍena hantuṃ punarabhyapadyata
translation: But Baka had to vomit out Kṛṣṇa, the son of a cowherd (Nanda), but (as a matter of fact) the father of god Brahmā, the creator of the world, as Kṛṣṇa burnt like fire the root of his palate. Finding him unhurt, Baka again attacked Kṛṣṇa with his bill, to kill him.

devanagari: तं आपतन्तं स निगृह्य तुण्डयोः । दोर्भ्यां बकं कंससखं सतां पतिः । पश्यत्सु बालेषु ददार लीलया । मुदावहो वीरणवद् दिवौकसाम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āpatantaṃ sa nigṛhya tuṇḍayoḥdorbhyāṃ bakaṃ kaṃsasakhaṃ satāṃ patiḥpaśyatsu bāleṣu dadāra līlayāmudāvaho vīraṇavad divaukasām
translation: As Baka, the friend of Kaṃsa, rushed at him, Kṛṣṇa, the protector of the righteous[3], caught hold of him by the two halves of the bill, and while the children were looking on, tore him up like a blade of grass in a sportive way, and brought delight to residents of the celestial world.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
v.l. satām gatiḥ ‘The final abode of the righteous’—VT.

devanagari: तदा बकारिं सुरलोकवासिनः । समाकिरन् नन्दनमल्लिकादिभिः । समीडिरे चानकशङ्खसंस्तवैः । तद्वीक्ष्य गोपालसुता विसिस्मिरे ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā bakāriṃ suralokavāsinaḥsamākiran nandanamallikādibhiḥsamīḍire cānakaśaṅkhasaṃstavaiḥtadvīkṣya gopālasutā visismire
translation: The denizens of heaven then showered the slayer of the demon Baka with jasmine and other flowers from the celestial garden Nandana, and praised him, expressing delight by blowing of conchs, and sounding of drums, beholding which the cowherd boys stood agape with amazement.

devanagari: मुक्तं बकास्याद् उपलभ्य बालका । रामादयः प्राणमिवेन्द्रियो गणः । स्थानागतं तं परिरभ्य निर्वृताः । प्रणीय वत्सान् व्रजमेत्य तज्जगुः ॥ ५३...
english_devnagari: muktaṃ bakāsyād upalabhya bālakārāmādayaḥ prāṇamivendriyo gaṇaḥsthānāgataṃ taṃ parirabhya nirvṛtāḥpraṇīya vatsān vrajametya tajjaguḥ
translation: On finding Kṛṣṇa safely released from the mouth of Baka and return to the playground, children like Balarāma and others, like unto senses would do to the vital breath, embraced ṃm and felt relieved and happy. Collecting their calves together, they returned to Vraja and reported (sang) that exploit (of Kṛṣṇa).

devanagari: श्रुत्वा तद् विस्मिता गोपा गोप्यश्चातिप्रियादृताः । प्रेत्य आगतमिवोत्सुक्याद् ऐक्षन्त तृषितेक्षणाः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā tad vismitā gopā gopyaścātipriyādṛtāḥpretya āgatamivotsukyād aikṣanta tṛṣitekṣaṇāḥ
translation: Astonished to hear it, Gopa men and women, who had an intensely loving regard for him, gazed him with eager (lit. thirsty) eyes, as if he had returned from the land of the dead.

devanagari: अहो बतास्य बालस्य बहवो मृत्यवोऽभवन् । अप्यासीद् विप्रियं तेषां कृतं पूर्वं यतो भयम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: aho batāsya bālasya bahavo mṛtyavo'bhavanapyāsīd vipriyaṃ teṣāṃ kṛtaṃ pūrvaṃ yato bhayam
translation: They said, “how wonderful yet pitiable that this child should have so many mortal enemies. However, it is they who suffered the evil consequences, as they spelt terror to others previously.

devanagari: अथापि अभिभवन्त्येनं नैव ते घोरदर्शनाः । जिघांसयैनमासाद्य नश्यन्त्यग्नौ पतङ्गवत् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi abhibhavantyenaṃ naiva te ghoradarśanāḥjighāṃsayainamāsādya naśyantyagnau pataṅgavat
translation: Howsoever terrible-looking the demons be, they dare not and cannot overpower him at all. Approaching him with a desire to kill, they themselves get destroyed like moths in fire.

devanagari: अहो ब्रह्मविदां वाचो नासत्याः सन्ति कर्हिचित् । गर्गो यदाह भगवान् अन्वभावि तथैव तत् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: aho brahmavidāṃ vāco nāsatyāḥ santi karhicitgargo yadāha bhagavān anvabhāvi tathaiva tat
translation: Oh! The words of those who are conversant with the Brahman, never prove to be untrue. What the worshipful Garga predicted has been exactly found to be true (by experience)

devanagari: इति नन्दादयो गोपाः कृष्णरामकथां मुदा । कुर्वन्तो रममाणाश्च नाविन्दन् भववेदनाम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti nandādayo gopāḥ kṛṣṇarāmakathāṃ mudākurvanto ramamāṇāśca nāvindan bhavavedanām
translation: In this way, recounting with joys the stories of Kṛṣṇa and Rāma, Nanda and other cowherds passed their time happily and did not experience the agonies of saṃsāra.

devanagari: एवं विहारैः कौमारैः कौमारं जहतुर्व्रजे । निलायनैः सेतुबन्धैः मर्कटोत्प्लवनादिभिः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vihāraiḥ kaumāraiḥ kaumāraṃ jahaturvrajenilāyanaiḥ setubandhaiḥ markaṭotplavanādibhiḥ
translation:
In this way, they (Kṛṣṇa and Rāma) passed their childhood at Vraja in child-like sports such as hide-and-seek, constructing dams in play, jumping like monkeys and the like.
[back to top]
